title: Vibe in Patriots building reportedly better than two weeks ago with Mike Vrabel
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Mike Vrabel returns to Patriots rookie minicamp amid offseason controversies but shows renewed energy and focus.
Mike Giardi reports improved atmosphere in Foxborough as Patriots prepare for a challenging 2026 season.
Newly acquired safety Kevin Byard praises Vrabel's coaching consistency, comparing him to his Tennessee days.

title: Willson Contreras’s go-ahead HR, Payton Tolle’s 8 strong innings lift Red Sox past Braves 3-2
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Willson Contreras hit a decisive 426-foot homer in the eighth inning, leading the Red Sox to a 3-2 victory.
Payton Tolle pitched eight innings, allowing two runs, four hits, and one walk, with three strikeouts against the Braves.
Aroldis Chapman secured the save after overcoming a bases-loaded situation in the ninth inning for the Red Sox.

title: Red Sox place Trevor Story on injured list with sports hernia, contemplating surgery
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Red Sox shortstop Trevor Story placed on 10-day injured list with a sports hernia.
Infielder Nick Sogard recalled from Triple-A Worcester following Story's placement on injured list.
Story might need surgery; the recovery could take 6-10 weeks, impacting his season.

title: One driver dead in NH collision between pickup truck and school bus
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: David Peloski, 74, of Swanzey, NH, died in a head-on collision between his pickup and a school bus in Marlborough.
The crash on Route 12 involved a school bus with 13 students, who were not seriously injured; some were hospitalized as a precaution.
School bus driver James French, 77, sustained non-life-threatening injuries; the incident is under investigation by New Hampshire State Police.

title: Carl Yastrzemski’s grandson, Braves’ Mike Yastrzemski, walks off Red Sox
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Mike Yastrzemski's walk-off hit in the 10th inning secured a 3-2 win for the Braves over the Red Sox.
Red Sox pitcher Connelly Early allowed two solo home runs in five innings but struck out six with no walks.
Boston's Marcelo Mayer tied the game in the seventh inning with a solo home run, his second of the season.

title: Report: Reunion between Patriots, Stefon Diggs viewed as unlikely despite mutual interest
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Stefon Diggs is interested in rejoining the Patriots, but an A.J. Brown trade complicates his potential return.
The Patriots would take on a $32 million AAV contract for Brown, limiting cap space available for Diggs.
Diggs, a top receiver last season, may not accept a reduced role if Brown and Doubs are prioritized.

title: Boston police seek missing boy, 15
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Boston police seek public help to find missing 15-year-old Lafayette LaGrant-Gomes, last seen near Blue Hill Avenue, Dorchester.
Lafayette was last seen Tuesday at 1 p.m. He is described as Black, 5'8", thin build, brown eyes, and black hair.
Authorities urge those with information to contact District B-3 detectives or use Boston Police CrimeStoppers for anonymous tips.

title: Police: Teen allegedly shot girlfriend’s mother in the head on Mother’s Day
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Tyler Broomes, 18, allegedly shot his girlfriend's mother and another man during an argument in Dorchester on Mother's Day.
The victims, both 42, suffered life-threatening and non-life-threatening injuries respectively; the incident occurred at 69 Kingsdale St.
Broomes faces multiple charges including assault with intent to murder; he remains held without bail pending a hearing.

title: How would a team made up entirely of former Red Sox look in 2026?
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Former Red Sox pitchers Sale, Eovaldi, Rodriguez, Wacha, and Harrison have a combined 2.74 ERA in 2026.
Kyle Schwarber leads MLB with 18 home runs and was available as a free agent this offseason.
Former Red Sox players Betts, Bogaerts, and Smith remain productive, emphasizing Boston's missed opportunities.

title: Red Sox’ Roman Anthony no longer wearing wrist brace, played catch Friday
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Red Sox player Roman Anthony has been out since May 7 with a right wrist sprain.
Anthony has removed his wrist brace and played catch but has not started swinging a bat yet.
Interim manager Chad Tracy hopes Anthony will start batting soon, depending on his recovery progress.
